pages.full_text:
Before looking at the Cathdral we had pizza from a real wood-fired oven. The pizza rested directly on the hot stones.

   On & to __Connantre__ for the night. Simon paddled in the lake, Eve swam & the girls watched. A long, sandy Beach.
                                  2 squatter
                                  bos

[glued-in newspaper clipping with image of person using long-handled paddle at wood-fired oven, caption reads: "Nous cuisons dans nos fours l'ensemble de nos pains pour qu'ils obtiennent une mie moelleuse et une croûte croquante et bien dorée."]

Tuesday
   We drove for 1 hour & shopped at Arcis-sur-Aube where Simon & Melody & Charlotte did a huge Supermarket Shop. The girls liked the Chaource cheese & Prawn. A long, long day's drive from 9.30-6.15 They swam at the small pool when we got here to Le Beau Sejour * * *.
   There are Chinese Students (management at Lyons) working as fruit packers, staying at the camp for 2 months, 2 of them from Shanghai. Also cars from Czech & Lithuania. I slept well. It's warmer.
photo_captions: Nous cuisons dans nos fours l'ensemble de nos pains pour qu'ils obtiennent une mie moelleuse et une croûte croquante et bien dorée.
pages.notes: Top of page is slightly cut off — no handwritten page number visible. The marginal annotation '2 squatter bos' is unclear; 'bos' may be an abbreviation. 'Connantre' could also read 'Connantre' or possibly 'Connantré' — a village in Marne, France. The newspaper clipping is in French and relates to wood-fired bread ovens. 'Chaource' cheese is a specialty of the Aube region, consistent with Arcis-sur-Aube. Year cannot be determined from this page alone; context suggests a French camping trip, volume B covers 2003–2019.

pages.full_text:
Wednesday                                                 57
   We left the motorway & went through Valreas in Provence, & reached N.D. des Vignes, an old pilgrimage site in a pine plantation. The nun said that the hermitage well had run dry & she wished they could have some rain from the English floods! She asked Melody & Charlotte if they could hear the grasshoppers

Reims Cathedral
1974 Chagall
left lancet
Virgin &
Child

[large glued-in colour photo of Chagall stained glass window depicting Virgin and Child]
photo_captions: Reims Cathedral 1974 Chagall left lancet Virgin & Child
pages.notes: The text ends mid-sentence with 'grasshoppers' — the sentence likely continues on the next page. 'N.D. des Vignes' is Notre-Dame des Vignes, a pilgrimage site. The photo caption is written beside the large Chagall stained glass photo and describes the Reims Cathedral Chagall window from 1974. The Wednesday entry seems to describe a different day/location from the Tuesday entry (Provence vs Aube region), suggesting pages may span multiple days of a longer trip. Year cannot be confirmed from content alone.
